units.topic_name_en: Future Forms: will / going to / present continuous
units.topic_name_ar: صيغ المستقبل: will و going to والمضارع المستمر للخطط

explanation_sections.content_ar:
● القاعدة
عندك ثلاث صيغ للمستقبل، وكل وحدة لها شغلة:
• going to = خطة قرّرتيها من قبل. مثال: We are going to focus on three channels.
• المضارع المستمر (am/is/are + فعل+ing) = ترتيب مؤكّد بموعد أو مكان محدّد. مثال: I am meeting the design team on Sunday.
• will = قرار في نفس اللحظة، أو توقّع، أو نتيجة شرط. مثال: If the results are strong, we will launch a second phase.
لاحظي إن الفرق مو في الزمن بس، الفرق في نوع الكلام: خطة، أو موعد مثبّت، أو توقّع. جرّبي تسألين نفسك «هذي خطة قرّرتها؟ ولا موعد محجوز؟ ولا مجرّد توقّع؟» وبتعرفين وش تستخدمين.
explanation_sections.content_en:
Future forms — three ways to talk about what's next:

• going to = a plan you already decided: We are going to focus on three channels.
• present continuous (am/is/are + verb-ing) = a fixed arrangement with a set time or place: I am meeting the design team on Sunday.
• will = an on-the-spot decision, a prediction, or the result of a condition: If the results are strong, we will launch a second phase.

The choice depends on the type of statement — a plan, a booked arrangement, or a prediction — not just the timing.

explanation_sections.content_ar:
● من مشهدك
هذي جمل من عرض استراتيجيتك «Everyday Glow»، شوفي كيف كل صيغة جت في مكانها:
• We are going to test two versions of each ad. (خطة قرّرتيها مع الفريق)
• I am presenting the full plan to the client next week. (موعد مثبّت في تقويمك)
• By the end of May, we will reach two million people. (رقم متوقّع)
explanation_sections.content_en:
From your Everyday Glow strategy:
• We are going to test two versions of each ad. (a decided plan)
• I am presenting the full plan to the client next week. (a fixed arrangement)
• By the end of May, we will reach two million people. (a prediction)

explanation_sections.content_ar:
● أخطاء شائعة
• ✗ We are going to launched a second phase. ✓ We are going to launch a second phase. — بعد going to يجي المصدر (launch) مو الماضي.
• ✗ I am meet the design team on Sunday. ✓ I am meeting the design team on Sunday. — المضارع المستمر لازم فعل+ing (meeting).
• ✗ Tomorrow I will to send the brief. ✓ Tomorrow I will send the brief. — بعد will يجي المصدر بدون to.
explanation_sections.content_en:
Watch out for these:
• ✗ We are going to launched a second phase. → ✓ We are going to launch a second phase. — after going to, use the base verb.
• ✗ I am meet the design team on Sunday. → ✓ I am meeting the design team on Sunday. — the present continuous needs verb + -ing.
• ✗ Tomorrow I will to send the brief. → ✓ Tomorrow I will send the brief. — after will, use the base verb with no to.

explanation_sections.content_ar:
● متى تستخدمينها
وأنتِ تعرضين، خلي القاعدة بسيطة:
• going to للخطط اللي قرّرتيها مع فريقك (الاستراتيجية، القنوات، المراحل).
• المضارع المستمر للمواعيد المثبّتة في تقويمك (اجتماع العميل، لقاء فريق التصميم).
• will للأرقام المتوقّعة والوعود ونتائج الشروط (الوصول المتوقّع، «لو النتايج قوية…»).
نصيحة نبرة: going to و will الاثنين مناسبين للعرض الرسمي؛ بس لا تستخدمين will لكل شي — لو كل خططك «will» بيحسّ العميل إنها لسا مو مؤكّدة.
explanation_sections.content_en:
Choosing in a pitch:
• Use going to for plans you and your team decided (the strategy, the channels, the phases).
• Use the present continuous for arrangements already in your calendar (the client meeting, the design review).
• Use will for expected numbers, promises, and results of conditions (projected reach, if the results are strong…).

Register tip: both going to and will suit a formal pitch — but if every plan is will, it can sound less settled. Reserve will for predictions and promises.

exercises.instructions_en: Put the verb in the correct future form.
exercises.instructions_ar: ضعي الفعل في صيغة المستقبل الصحيحة

item.question: We've already decided our approach: we ______ (run) the influencer teasers before the official launch.
item.correct_answer: are going to run
item.explanation_ar: قرار قرّرتيه من قبل مع الفريق ← going to + مصدر.
item.instruction_ar: ضعي الفعل في صيغة المستقبل الصحيحة

exercises.instructions_en: Put the verb in the correct future form.
exercises.instructions_ar: ضعي الفعل في صيغة المستقبل الصحيحة

item.question: It's fixed in my calendar — I ______ (pitch) the campaign to the client at 10 on Sunday.
item.correct_answer: am pitching
item.explanation_ar: موعد مثبّت في التقويم ← المضارع المستمر (am + -ing).
item.instruction_ar: ضعي الفعل في صيغة المستقبل الصحيحة

exercises.instructions_en: Put the verb in the correct future form.
exercises.instructions_ar: ضعي الفعل في صيغة المستقبل الصحيحة

item.question: If the reach is high enough, the client ______ (approve) the summer budget.
item.correct_answer: will approve
item.explanation_ar: نتيجة مبنية على شرط ← will + مصدر.
item.instruction_ar: ضعي الفعل في صيغة المستقبل الصحيحة

item.question: I can't make it later — I ______ the design team at noon; it's already booked.
item.correct_answer: am meeting
item.explanation_ar: موعد محجوز بوقت محدّد ← المضارع المستمر (am meeting).

item.question: Don't worry about the second phase now — if sales grow, we ______ it in the summer.
item.correct_answer: will launch
item.explanation_ar: نتيجة شرط غير مؤكّد، مو خطة مثبّتة ← will launch.

item.question: Rewrite using 'going to': We plan to test two versions of each ad.
item.correct_answer: We are going to test two versions of each ad.
item.explanation_ar: خطة مقرّرة ← be going to + مصدر.

item.question: Rewrite as a present continuous arrangement: I have a meeting with the client next Tuesday.
item.correct_answer: I am meeting the client next Tuesday.
item.explanation_ar: ترتيب مثبّت بموعد ← المضارع المستمر.

item.question: Find and correct the error: 'We are going to scaling the version with the higher engagement.'
item.correct_answer: We are going to scale the version with the higher engagement.
item.explanation_ar: بعد going to نستخدم المصدر ← scale.

item.question: Find and correct the error: 'By the end of May, we will reaching two million people.'
item.correct_answer: By the end of May, we will reach two million people.
item.explanation_ar: بعد will نستخدم المصدر بدون ing ← reach.

exercises.instructions_en: Put the words in the correct order.

item.question: Put the words in order: client / We / presenting / the / are / strategy / to / the / next / week
item.correct_answer: We are presenting the strategy to the client next week.
item.explanation_ar: ترتيب مثبّت قريب ← المضارع المستمر.

units.topic_name_en: Modals of Obligation: must / have to / should / need to
units.topic_name_ar: أفعال الإلزام: must و have to و should و need to

explanation_sections.content_ar:
● القاعدة
لما تمرّرين موجز، فرّقي بين درجات الإلزام:
• must / have to = إلزام قوي، «لازم». مثال: Everyone must read the guidelines.
• need to = ضرورة عملية عشان الشغل يمشي. مثال: We need to deliver the drafts by Thursday.
• should = نصيحة أو الخيار الأنسب، مو إلزام. مثال: The tone should be warm.
• don't have to = مو لازم، اختياري. مثال: We don't have to translate everything.
• shouldn't = يُفضَّل تتجنّبينه. مثال: They shouldn't use other shades.
لاحظي: بعد must و should و shouldn't يجي المصدر بدون to (must read، should be)، أما have to و need to أصلاً فيهم to.
explanation_sections.content_en:
Levels of obligation in a brief:
• must / have to = strong obligation (you have no choice): Everyone must read the guidelines.
• need to = practical necessity to get the work done: We need to deliver the drafts by Thursday.
• should = advice or the better choice, not a rule: The tone should be warm.
• don't have to = optional, no obligation: We don't have to translate everything.
• shouldn't = advised against: They shouldn't use other shades.

After must / should / shouldn't use the base verb (no to); have to and need to already contain to.

explanation_sections.content_ar:
● من مشهدك
من موجز حملة «Moms' Time»، شوفي كيف تفرّق النبرة بين لازم واختياري:
• The designers must follow the brand colors exactly. (إلزام)
• Copywriters need to write short captions — under twenty words. (ضرورة عملية)
• We don't have to translate everything, but the slogan has to work in both languages. (اختياري + إلزام في نفس الجملة)
explanation_sections.content_en:
From your Moms' Time brief:
• The designers must follow the brand colors exactly. (obligation)
• Copywriters need to write short captions — under twenty words. (practical necessity)
• We don't have to translate everything, but the slogan has to work in both languages. (optional + obligation in one line)

explanation_sections.content_ar:
● أخطاء شائعة
• ✗ Everyone must to read the guidelines. ✓ Everyone must read the guidelines. — بعد must يجي المصدر بدون to.
• ✗ The designer must follows the brand colors. ✓ The designer must follow the brand colors. — بعد الفعل الناقص ما نحط s، نستخدم المصدر.
• ✗ We don't must translate everything. ✓ We don't have to translate everything. — «مو لازم» تجي don't have to، مو «don't must».
explanation_sections.content_en:
Watch out for these:
• ✗ Everyone must to read the guidelines. → ✓ Everyone must read the guidelines. — after must, use the base verb with no to.
• ✗ The designer must follows the brand colors. → ✓ The designer must follow the brand colors. — no -s after a modal; use the base verb.
• ✗ We don't must translate everything. → ✓ We don't have to translate everything. — for 'not necessary' use don't have to, never 'don't must'.

explanation_sections.content_ar:
● متى تستخدمينها
عشان فريقك يعرف الأولويات من أول نظرة:
• استخدمي must / have to / need to للأشياء اللي ما فيها تفاوض (رسالة العميل، الموعد النهائي، ألوان البراند).
• استخدمي should للتوجيه والذوق (النبرة، طول التعليق) — تعطينهم مساحة بدون ما تفرضين.
• استخدمي don't have to عشان تريحينهم من شي يظنّونه إلزامي (مثلاً الترجمة الكاملة).
فرق النبرة: must أقوى وأرسمية، have to أقرب للكلام اليومي، need to ألطف وتحسّها تعاون. مع فريقك، need to و should يخلّون التوجيه حازم وودود في نفس الوقت.
explanation_sections.content_en:
So your team sees the priorities at a glance:
• Use must / have to / need to for the non-negotiables (the client's message, the deadline, the brand colors).
• Use should for guidance and taste (tone, caption length) — it gives room without forcing.
• Use don't have to to lift a burden they assume is required (like full translation).

Register: must is the strongest and most formal, have to is more everyday, and need to feels softer and collaborative. With your team, need to and should keep your direction firm yet warm.

exercises.instructions_en: Fill in the correct modal plus the verb in brackets.
exercises.instructions_ar: ضعي الفعل الناقص المناسب مع الفعل بين القوسين حسب المعنى

item.question: Everyone ______ (read) the client's guidelines before we start — this is non-negotiable. (strong obligation)
item.correct_answer: must read
item.explanation_ar: إلزام قوي بدون تفاوض ← must + مصدر بدون to.
item.instruction_ar: ضعي الفعل الناقص المناسب مع الفعل بين القوسين حسب المعنى

exercises.instructions_en: Fill in the correct modal plus the verb in brackets.
exercises.instructions_ar: ضعي الفعل الناقص المناسب مع الفعل بين القوسين حسب المعنى

item.question: We ______ (deliver) the first drafts by Thursday, so plan your week carefully. (practical necessity)
item.correct_answer: need to deliver
item.explanation_ar: ضرورة عملية عشان الشغل يمشي ← need to + مصدر.
item.instruction_ar: ضعي الفعل الناقص المناسب مع الفعل بين القوسين حسب المعنى

exercises.instructions_en: Fill in the correct modal plus the verb in brackets.
exercises.instructions_ar: ضعي الفعل الناقص المناسب مع الفعل بين القوسين حسب المعنى

item.question: You ______ (translate) every caption into English — it's optional this time. (no obligation)
item.correct_answer: don't have to translate
item.explanation_ar: اختياري مو إلزامي ← don't have to + مصدر.
item.instruction_ar: ضعي الفعل الناقص المناسب مع الفعل بين القوسين حسب المعنى

item.question: The tone ______ be warm and friendly, not formal — that's my advice, not a rule.
item.correct_answer: should
item.explanation_ar: نصيحة مو إلزام ← should.

item.question: The main slogan ______ work in both Arabic and English — the client requires it.
item.correct_answer: has to
item.explanation_ar: إلزام من العميل ← has to.

item.question: Rewrite so it means it is NOT necessary: You have to translate the whole campaign.
item.correct_answer: You don't have to translate the whole campaign.
item.explanation_ar: نفي الإلزام (مو لازم) ← don't have to.

item.question: Rewrite as advice using 'should': It's a good idea to ask me about the scope today.
item.correct_answer: You should ask me about the scope today.
item.explanation_ar: نصيحة ← should + مصدر.

item.question: Find and correct the error: 'Everyone must to read the client's feedback before the meeting.'
item.correct_answer: Everyone must read the client's feedback before the meeting.
item.explanation_ar: بعد must نستخدم المصدر بدون to ← read.

item.question: Find and correct the error: 'The designers must follows the brand colors exactly.'
item.correct_answer: The designers must follow the brand colors exactly.
item.explanation_ar: بعد الفعل الناقص لا نضيف s ← follow.

exercises.instructions_en: Put the words in the correct order.

item.question: Put the words in order: message / You / to / keep / have / the / in / caption / every
item.correct_answer: You have to keep the message in every caption.
item.explanation_ar: إلزام ← have to + مصدر، وبعدها بقية الجملة.
